raw_input:
[5.0/5] Badger Sunscreen For the past many years, Badger is the only sunscreen I use as it is highly rated by the EWG (Environmental Working Group) that tests and rates all sunscreens.

This purchase is the first time I have used the Damascus Rose scented version as I try to stay away from heavy fragrances; however, this is a very light, pleasant scent. This sunscreen also seems much smoother and creamier than the regular version which I appreciate since it goes on the face.

I will not hesitate to buy this sunscreen again. [5.0/5] Lovely product Love this Badger product. I used this as my everyday sunscreen for face and décolleté. Blends in well and is non greasy. The scent is lovely, natural and light. I am VERY picky about anything I put on my face as I have sensitive skin - this product ticks all the boxes for a light, wearable, everyday sunscreen. Badger also makes a 20% Damascus Rose sunscreen. I am switching to that because it has a higher SPF yet feels even lighter on my face. Either way you can't go wrong with the Damascus Rose Face Sunscreen! [5.0/5] AMAZING! Makes your skin bright and beautiful. The smell is earthy and instantly relaxing and one of the best qualities of this product. It's very soft, therefore it applies to your skin like "butter." I fell in love with this product as soon as I used it the first time and that doesn't usually happen very often. A lot of products have amazing ratings and then can be very dissapointing but not with this one. This is definitely my new daily sunscreen lotion. The Badger company did a very good job with this product. [5.0/5] Nice SPF for your Face (with safe ingredients). I have photosensitivity, so I use a lot of sunscreen. Because of that I have looked into safe brands. Badger is my favorite. However, I had been using their regular sunscreens on my body as well as my face, and realized maybe I should try this one that is specifically for the face. I'm glad I did.

Zinc oxide is a safe sun screen ingredient. The downside is that it can leave a white-ish residue on your skin. I found with Badger's (and all brands using zinc oxide) this was true. I am very pale so it's not a huge issue to me. And I solved it best by exfoliating my face a few times a week (the white tint seems to grab more onto dry patches) and also after putting on the sun screen, I use that mineral powder makeup and I just tap on a little of that to get my regular looking skin color back. But with the regular badger sun screens, like most with zinc oxide, you really have to rub it into your skin. It sort of starts to "melt" a bit and smooth out, and on my body it's no big deal. But on my face, with sensitive skin, I like to rub it as little as possible.

And that is where this facial sun screen lotion comes in perfect. It is easier to smooth onto your face. It is creamier and lighter in texture. There is a slight white-ish hue, especuially if you overdue it, but it seems less than when using the other bigger tubes of Badger sunscreen. And it feels nice on my skin and leaves if feeling soft afterwards.

This is my first bottle of this lotion and I find the rose scent to be faint. I smell it just slightly, sort of mixed with a slight clean, herbal sort of scent. And it fades away soon enough. I saw other reviews state the rose smell was too heavy or smelt like a grandmother, so I am not sure who's bottle is the norm, or who got a bottle that is unusual. I hope I didn't get an old bottle. But it seems to be working as far as sun protection. i would love if it smelled a bit more like rose because I love roses and their scent. It is real rose. Badger does not use fake fragrance.

This is now what I will be using on my face year long. (Unless they come out with a facial lotion with SPF 30, which would be great.) [2.0/5] Okay, but keep searching This cream looked old when I received it. The pump stopped working every two pumps. Even when I unscrewed the cap and moved it around, the pump just didn't work. I've only used it a week, and now the pump has completely stopped working. I just take off the cap, but the stick is so tiny that it's not that great at removing the sunscreen. UGH!!! Not worth the money. If it works, I wouldn't know. I can barely get enough out to use. I would give it a zero for usability, but since it didn't make me break out, I think two stars is fair. [2.0/5] Left a white cast on my skin I was super excited about this product and thrilled with the ingredients, but I did not like the white sheen it left on my skin. I looked like I was ready for kabuki theater. Gave it to my friend with a different skin type than me. I have older skin (43) that is dry and rather thick (I have to exfoliate a lot). I trust the other reviewers. I must have weird skin! [5.0/5] Skin Saver I'm a big fan of Badger's Cocoa Butter Lip Balms, and this sunscreen did not disappoint. It glides on and smells great in the process, and definitely saved me from getting a sunburn on my face when I was on a trip. I didn't put any sunscreen on my shoulders or arms and got a horrible sunburn but my face remained fine! I would definitely recommend this, it feels lightweight yet moisturizing, and I didn't feel like I was using sunscreen at all. Since it's broad spectrum it might appear to have a whitish cast at first, but within seconds that disappears. [5.0/5] Fancy Sunscreen This facial sunscreen has a great smell and blends very well, without leaving white streaks behind. It didn't make my face oily and I felt that it made my skin look more even toned. I would like it better if some of the antioxidants were higher up on the ingredients' list but not if it compromised the consistency. Overall, I'm pleased with this Badger product, I'm going to have check some of their other stuff out. [4.0/5] ingredients list Why do I love this sunscreen? This one, unlike the normal badger sunscreen, does not make people stare at me. Dermatologists say that you should use a pea sized amount of sunscreen to cover your face. Try as I might, no matter how much I blended it, the original sunscreen would always give me a white cast.

Well, this sunscreen actually goes on invisible! It blends dreamily, no more smushing zinc bits around like in the original formula, and this one has great antioxidants in it!

Full ingredients list below, direct from the Badger website:

Active Sunscreen Ingredient:
Non-Nano, Uncoated Zinc Oxide 10.55%

Other Ingredients:
*Aloe Barbadensis (Organic Aloe) Extract, *Olea Europaea (Organic Extra Virgin Olive) Oil, *Sucrose Polysoyate (from Organic Soybean), *Ethyl Palmate (from Organic Palm), Aqua (Purified Water), *Sucrose Cocoate (from Organic Coconut), *Glycerin (from Organic Palm), *Citrus Medica Limonum (Organic Lemon) Extract, Chondrus Crispus (Carrageenan) Extract, Xanthan Gum, *Lavandula Angustifolia (Organic Lavender) Oil, *Hydrolyzed Soy Protein (from Organic Soybean), *Rosa Canina (Organic Rosehip) CO2 Extract, *Hippophae Rhamnoides (Organic Seabuckthorn) CO2 Extract, *Rosa Damascena (Organic Rose Otto) Oil, *Anthemis Nobilis (Organic Roman Chamomile) Oil, and *Calendula Officinalis (Organic Calendula) CO2 Extract.

* = Certified Organic
Total Organic Content (excluding water): 75.31% [5.0/5] Awesome Product!!! Smells good, goes on clear, doesnt clog pores, and provides protection from the sun using non nano zinc!!!! Best facial moisturizer around! [3.0/5] Good protection, but bad scent! I tried this sunscreen because of the great score it received on the EWG sunscreen report. Everything about it would be great if it wasn't scented. I mean no offense to anyone, but it smells very old ladyish to me. The scent doesn't necessarily fade like I had hoped it would! I will continue my search for a vitamin A-free, daily moisturizing facial lotion sunscreen due to the fragrance of this product! [3.0/5] Not bad I was looking for a reasonably priced moisturizer that has sunscreen in it. Based on reviews I decided to give this product a try. It wasn't bad. A little heavy but blends in well. Has a little bit of a funny odor but I got used to it and didn't really notice it after a little while using it. I have since started using Juice Beauty which I like a lot better and seems to last me longer and is more economical. [5.0/5] wonderful face spf. excellent product! love the natural ingredients. used for a week in hawaii, and my face never burned. it doesn't irritate my skin at all, and the smell is light. will buy again. [4.0/5] Antioxidant face lotion combined with broad spectrum sun protection I have been looking for a broad spectrum daily face lotion/sunscreen for a long time. I read through all 27 "Best Moisturizers with SPF" on the Environmental Working Group's 2012 list and this Badger one seems promising.
--- INGREDIENTS ---
The lotion aspect is packed with all natural plant derived ingredients some with antioxidant abilities. The sun screen aspect provides broad spectrum UVA/UVB protection through a physical agent zinc oxide not a chemical agent; further, the zinc oxide used are non-nano sized at about 600nm and the Badger website provides a chart showing the size distributions of their ZnO particles.
--- USAGE ---
In terms of usage, I have been using this every other day for about a week. It glides on smoothly and has a light pleasant fragrance. It makes my skin feel soft and does not make my face whitish at all.
--- EFFECTIVENESS ---
I have not been exposed to the sun for more than 20 minutes at a time yet while having this product on my face. Will try to update review once I have more data.

I am slightly concerned with the relatively low SPF (16) compared to other daily sunscreens hence the 4 stars. But at the same time I did not want to have loads of sunscreen agents pasted on my face all day. So it's a trade-off.

Another product that may be promising with higher SPF (30) is Suntegrity Skincare All Natural Moisturizing Face Protection. However its ZnO particles are smaller at 220nm, but still non-nano by a long shot. Also it's about 1.5 times the unit price as this Badger one.

The sun screen aspect provides broad spectrum UVA/UVB protection through a physical agent zinc oxide not a chemical agent; further, the zinc oxide used are non-nano sized at about 600nm and the Badger website provides a chart showing the size distributions of their ZnO particles.

response:
Badger Damascus Rose face sunscreen get many good reviews. Most people love smell - light, natural rose scent. Few say smell too strong, like old lady. 

Product go on smooth like butter, feel creamy, not greasy. Most say no white cast or very little. Two people say white cast bad problem - one look like kabuki theater. Person with dry thick skin have worst trouble.

EWG rate product high - many buyers choose for that reason. Ingredients all natural, organic. Active ingredient non-nano zinc oxide 10.55%. No fake fragrance.

Work good as sunscreen - one person burn shoulders but face stay fine whole trip. Good for sensitive skin. Feel lightweight, moisturizing.

Bad reviews mention pump broken fast, product look old on arrival. Some say SPF 16 too low for daily use. One person switch to Juice Beauty instead.

Overall most buyers happy, buy again. Good for face and sensitive skin. Badger also make 20% Damascus Rose version with higher SPF for those wanting more protection.
